Bani.milk_yield: Average ~2,857 kg per lactation (range ~1,095–6,054 kg) with fat ~6.65% and daily yield ~12-19 litres under good conditions
Bani.special_features: Well-adapted to arid grazing land, tolerant to drought and extreme heat, with unique tightly coiled horns and strong grazing stamina

Bhadawari.milk_yield: Average ~1,294 kg per lactation (range ~540-1,400 kg), with fat content ~6-12.8% (average ~7.9%)
Bhadawari.special_features: Good adaptation to ravines and coarse fodder; distinct copper-brown colour with two white lines on lower neck; known for high butter-fat milk rather than high volume

Jaffarabadi.milk_yield: Average ~2,239 kg per lactation (~305 days) with butterfat ~7.7%
Jaffarabadi.special_features: Heaviest Indian buffalo breed, large heavy horns drooping sideways then curving up, very efficient roughage converter, native to Saurashtra region of Gujarat

Mehsana.milk_yield: Average ~1,988 kg per lactation (range ~598-3,597 kg) with fat ~6.8% (range ~5.2-9.5%)
Mehsana.special_features: Evolved from crosses of Murrah buffalo and Surti buffalo; jet-black coat, sickle-shaped horns curving upward, good lactation persistency and regular breeding

Murrah.milk_yield: Average ~1,752 kg per lactation (range ~1,003–2,057 kg) with fat ~7.3% according to one source; under good management can yield ~15-20 litres/day at peak. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
Murrah.body_weight_male: Approximately 550 kg (adult bull) under typical conditions. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}
Murrah.body_weight_female: Approximately 450 kg (adult cow) under typical conditions. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}
Murrah.special_features: Jet-black colour (sometimes with white markings), short tightly curved horns, high milk fat content, excellent global reputation for dairying. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

Nili Ravi.milk_yield: Approximately 1,800-2,300 kg per lactation (305 days corrected mean ~2,247 kg) with fat ~6.8%-7%. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
Nili Ravi.body_weight_male: Around ~700-800 kg (mature bull) under good conditions. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}
Nili Ravi.body_weight_female: Around ~525-600 kg (mature cow) under good conditions. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}
Nili Ravi.special_features: Black (sometimes brown) coat often with white markings on forehead, face, muzzle, legs and tail (called “Panch Kalyani”); tightly-curled horns; one of the premier milch river-type buffalo breeds from the Punjab region. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

Surti.milk_yield: Varies by management; typically medium to high
Surti.special_features: Known for regional adaptability and distinct physical traits

Toda.milk_yield: Around ~500 kg per lactation, average fat ~8.2%-8.7%
Toda.body_weight_male: Up to ~700 kg (reported) under native conditions
Toda.body_weight_female: Up to ~620 kg (reported) under native conditions
Toda.special_features: Small, indigenous to Nilgiri Hills (Tamil Nadu), ash-grey/fawn coat, long outward-curving horns, used in tribal rituals and agriculture work

Alambadi.milk_yield: ≈ 432 kg per lactation (average): daily ~2.6 kg, lactation length ~171 days
Alambadi.special_features: Indigenous draught cattle of Tamil Nadu (hilly tract of Dharmapuri/Krishnagiri); iron-grey coat with white patches; good endurance under harsh grazing conditions

Amritmahal.milk_yield: Low to medium; usually 500–600 kg per lactation
Amritmahal.special_features: Famous Mysore draught breed with high endurance, narrow face, long horns, and strong pulling power; well suited for dry regions

Ayrshire.milk_yield: Typically 5,000–7,000 litres per lactation (305 days), good fat and protein levels
Ayrshire.special_features: Hardy dairy breed from Scotland, red-and-white coat, good grazing ability, strong feet and legs, suited for both cold and warm climates

Bargur.milk_yield: About 350 kg per lactation (range approx. 250-1,300 kg) under native conditions
Bargur.body_weight_male: Approximately 300-340 kg (mature bull under native conditions)
Bargur.body_weight_female: Approximately 250-295 kg (mature cow under native conditions)
Bargur.special_features: Compact hill-draught breed native to the Bargur Hills (Tamil Nadu); reddish-brown with white patches, well adapted to rugged terrain, known for endurance and trotting ability

Brown Swiss.milk_yield: Usually 6,000–9,000 litres per lactation (305 days) with fat around 4% and protein around 3.5%
Brown Swiss.special_features: Strong, hardy dairy breed from Switzerland; known for quiet temperament, good grazing ability, strong legs and hooves, and high protein-to-fat ratio in milk

Dangi.milk_yield: Approximately 430 kg per lactation on average (range ~175-800 kg) with average fat around 4.3% :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
Dangi.body_weight_male: Data less well-documented; medium size, bulls estimated at around 450-550 kg mature weight
Dangi.body_weight_female: Again not well documented; cows likely in the range of ~300-400 kg under native conditions
Dangi.special_features: White coat with irregular red or black spots, short thick horns, skin secretes an oily substance that helps cope with heavy rainfall/hilly terrain; well adapted draft breed in heavy-rainfall rice-field and forest hill zones of western India :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

Deoni.milk_yield: Average ~868 kg per lactation (range ~638–1,229 kg) with fat ~4.3% in typical field conditions. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
Deoni.body_weight_male: Reported mature bulls ~590 kg, height ~135-140 cm. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}
Deoni.body_weight_female: Mature cows approx. ~286 kg (based on measured sample) under field conditions. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}
Deoni.special_features: Dual-purpose breed (milk + draught) from Maharashtra/Karnataka drought-prone zones; three colour strains (Balankya, Wannera, Shevera); hardy and adapted to dry lands. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

Gir.milk_yield: Average ~2,063 litres per lactation (326 days) with ~4.7-5% fat. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
Gir.special_features: Milch breed from Gujarat, very heat-tolerant, resilient under tropical stress, distinct convex forehead and long pendulous ears. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

Guernsey.milk_yield: Around ~6,000 kg per lactation (305 days) with butter-fat about ~5% and protein ~3.7% in typical conditions. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
Guernsey.special_features: Medium-sized dairy breed from the Channel Island of Guernsey, known for golden-tinged milk rich in β-carotene, efficient feed-to-milk conversion and ease of calving. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

Hallikar.milk_yield: Generally low; about 540–700 kg per lactation under native conditions.
Hallikar.special_features: Famous draught breed from Karnataka, very strong and fast, long sweeping horns, excellent endurance for ploughing and cart work.

Hariana.milk_yield: About 1,600–2,000 kg per lactation with fat around 4–5%.
Hariana.special_features: Dual-purpose North Indian breed known for good draught strength, heat tolerance, and disease resistance.

Holstein Friesian.milk_yield: Usually 7,000–12,000 litres per lactation (305 days) under good management; fat around 3.6–4%.
Holstein Friesian.special_features: World’s highest milk-producing dairy breed; black-and-white coat, large frame, strong udder capacity, widely used for commercial dairy farming.

Jersey.milk_yield: Typically 4,000–6,000 litres per lactation (305 days) with high butter-fat around 4.8–5.5%.
Jersey.special_features: Small, efficient dairy breed known for rich milk, easy calving, heat tolerance, and strong feed-to-milk conversion.

Kangayam.milk_yield: Low; usually around 540–700 kg per lactation under native conditions.
Kangayam.special_features: Strong draught breed from Tamil Nadu, silver-grey coat, sturdy build, excellent for carting and ploughing, very hardy in dry regions.

Kankrej.milk_yield: Around 1,700–2,500 kg per lactation with fat about 4–5%.
Kankrej.special_features: Large dual-purpose breed from Gujarat–Rajasthan; lyre-shaped horns, strong draught ability, heat-tolerant, and known for good endurance.

Kasargod.milk_yield: Low; usually around 400–500 kg per lactation under native conditions.
Kasargod.special_features: Small indigenous breed from Kerala; hardy, survives on poor feed, good disease resistance, and well suited for hilly, humid coastal regions.

Kenkatha.milk_yield: Low; generally around 500–600 kg per lactation under native conditions.
Kenkatha.special_features: Hardy draught breed from Uttar Pradesh–Madhya Pradesh border region; compact body, strong legs, well suited for dry and rocky terrain.

Khillari.milk_yield: Low; usually around 450–500 kg per lactation under native conditions.
Khillari.special_features: Well-known draught breed from Maharashtra and Karnataka; compact muscular build, long backward-curving horns, excellent stamina and suited for dry, harsh terrain.

Krishna Valley.milk_yield: Medium; usually around 900–1,200 kg per lactation under field conditions.
Krishna Valley.special_features: Large, powerful draught breed from Karnataka–Maharashtra region; grey coat, massive frame, strong yoke power, suited for heavy agricultural work.

Nagori.milk_yield: Low; generally around 500–600 kg per lactation under native conditions.
Nagori.special_features: Fast, strong draught breed from Rajasthan; light grey to white coat, long legs, excellent for carting and field work, very hardy in hot desert climate.

Nimari.milk_yield: Low to medium; usually around 600–800 kg per lactation under native conditions.
Nimari.special_features: Indigenous breed from Madhya Pradesh; red or red-spotted coat, good draught ability, hardy and well suited to hot, dry regions.

Ongole.milk_yield: Medium; usually around 1,000–1,500 litres per lactation with fat about 4–5%.
Ongole.special_features: Large, strong breed from Andhra Pradesh; known for disease resistance, heat tolerance, prominent hump, and used for both draught work and breeding (exported globally).

Pulikulam.milk_yield: Low; usually around 400–500 kg per lactation under native conditions.
Pulikulam.special_features: Indigenous draught and multipurpose breed from Tamil Nadu; known for agility, drought tolerance, strong survival ability, and commonly used in traditional Jallikattu.

Rathi.milk_yield: Usually around 1,560–2,000 litres per lactation with fat about 4.3–5%.
Rathi.special_features: Milch breed from Rajasthan; good heat tolerance, calm temperament, regular breeder, and efficient milk producer even in arid conditions.

Red Dane.milk_yield: Typically around 5,000–6,000 litres per lactation (305 days) with fat about 4–4.5%.
Red Dane.special_features: Dairy breed developed from Danish Red cattle; known for good milk solids, calm nature, strong build, and good fertility.

Red Sindhi.milk_yield: Usually around 1,700–2,300 litres per lactation with fat about 4.5–5.5%.
Red Sindhi.special_features: Popular milch breed from Pakistan–India region; copper-red coat, high heat tolerance, disease resistance, and good milk production even in harsh climates.

Sahiwal.milk_yield: Usually around 2,000–3,000 litres per lactation with fat about 4.5–5%.
Sahiwal.special_features: One of the best tropical dairy breeds; very heat tolerant, disease resistant, calm temperament, and high lactation persistence.

Tharparkar.milk_yield: Usually around 1,800–2,600 litres per lactation with fat about 4–5%.
Tharparkar.special_features: Dual-purpose breed from Rajasthan; white to light grey coat, very hardy, drought tolerant, and well adapted to arid desert conditions.

Umblachery.milk_yield: Usually around 600–800 kg per lactation under native conditions.
Umblachery.special_features: Indigenous draught breed from Tamil Nadu; compact body, dark grey to black coat, strong for ploughing and carting, and well adapted to humid coastal regions.

Vechur.milk_yield: Usually around 400–500 litres per lactation with fat about 5–6%.
Vechur.special_features: Smallest cattle breed in the world; extremely low maintenance, heat tolerant, resistant to many diseases, and produces highly nutritious milk.
